what_it_does: Defines a 24 by 24 SVG line icon showing a stacked tile or layered diamond shape with two small internal crossing strokes.
observations: Uses an inline SVG element with viewBox 0 0 24 24.
Sets fill to none and stroke to currentColor.
Uses stroke width 1.5 with round line caps and joins.
Contains three path elements: a top diamond tile, a lower stacked layer line, and two short internal detail strokes.
standouts: The icon is color-adaptive through currentColor.
The stacked shape is expressed with minimal geometric paths.
Internal crossing strokes add surface detail to the top tile.
conventions: Follows common outline-icon conventions: no fill, currentColor stroke, rounded stroke endpoints, 24px coordinate system.
Uses compact inline SVG formatting with no whitespace between elements.
concerns: No title or desc metadata is included for an accessible text label.
notes: A compact outline icon for representing stacked tiles, layers, or grouped surfaces.
questions: Should this icon have an accessible title when used directly inline?
Are the internal crossing strokes intended to imply tile texture, overlap, or placement guides?
purpose_vs_actual: The filename suggests a tile-stack icon, and the SVG depicts layered diamond-like tiles.
intention_signals: It seems intended as a reusable design-system icon that communicates stacked tiles or layered surfaces without embedding fixed color.
novelty: The short crossed interior strokes give the tile surface a distinct detail beyond the outer stacked silhouette.
contribution: Contributes a small currentColor line icon that represents stacked tiles or layers using simple stroked geometry.
summary_for_embedding: This is a 24x24 currentColor SVG outline icon depicting stacked diamond-shaped tiles with minimal internal surface detail.
intention_for_embedding: This was for reusable design-system iconography representing tile stacks, layers, or grouped surfaces.
